rag_rules: Place AGREE assumptions and guarantees in valid component type scopes for ports, and in implementation scopes for bindings after subcomponents and connections.
Use visible feature names and concrete bounds when defining range assumptions and guarantees in component type contracts.
generic_rules: Ensure AGREE annex blocks are placed in appropriate AADL scopes to maintain architectural integrity.
Validate that helper declarations match the types of expressions used in contracts.
Use named constants or visible relations when specifying numeric ranges in AGREE contracts.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Declare boolean helper variables for range checks using 'and' logic: eq var : bool = x >= min and x <= max;
Place annex agree blocks in component type scopes for port-level contracts, and in implementation scopes for binding-level contracts.
anti_patterns: Avoid placing implementation-level bindings in component type scopes; use implementation scopes instead.
Do not reference subcomponent ports directly in component type contracts without proper variable representation.
